examples.title: Demonstrating Memory Segments
examples.description: Program showing addresses of variables in different memory segments
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates different memory segments by printing addresses of various variables. Global and static variables will have lower addresses (Data/BSS), while local variables will have higher addresses (Stack). Dynamic memory will have addresses between Stack and Heap.

examples.title: Stack Overflow Demonstration
examples.description: Program showing stack overflow due to excessive recursion
examples.explanation: This demonstrates stack overflow by recursive function calls. Each recursion uses stack space for local arrays (~4KB each). The addresses decrease as Stack grows downward. Eventually the Stack collides with Heap or exceeds its limit, causing segmentation fault.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is recursion depth

examples.title: Memory Leak Demonstration
examples.description: Program showing memory leak due to unfreed heap memory
examples.explanation: This demonstrates memory leak in C. Memory allocated with malloc() on the Heap is not automatically freed when the function returns. The pointer to the allocated memory is lost when the function exits, making the memory inaccessible but still allocated. This wastes system memory until program termination.

examples.title: Heap fragmentation example
examples.explanation: This demonstrates heap fragmentation. When memory is allocated and freed in alternating patterns, the free blocks become scattered (external fragmentation). While total free memory may be sufficient, there may not be a contiguous block large enough for a new allocation, causing allocation failures.
